scope_boundary.allowed_now: Inventory old accident regression classes and verifier requirements before schema/protocol barriers.
scope_boundary.not_allowed_now: Do not implement guard conversion, compiler conversion, runtime verifier, schemas, registries, or using-control-json.

old_accident_surfaces.current_markdown_surface: Markdown requirements/design/goal/plan/review fixtures plus a small requirements.control.json sidecar.
old_accident_surfaces.json_port_groundwork: Port the approved answer method, forbidden substitute key, required path coverage, and design-answer-method review check into first-class JSON fields instead of Markdown section text.

old_accident_surfaces.current_markdown_surface: Markdown execution policy fixtures encode task level, work assignment, selected workflow, wave matrix, locks, context pack, return format, and integration checks.
old_accident_surfaces.json_port_groundwork: Represent work assignment, selected workflow, subagent execution mode, wave barriers, lock ownership, context pack, return format, and integration checks as JSON contract fields.

old_accident_surfaces.current_markdown_surface: Markdown goal and plan fixtures carry Steps That Make The Result True, required step ids, transition evidence, and supporting-only progress rules.
old_accident_surfaces.json_port_groundwork: Make required steps, state transitions, transition evidence, mainline/supporting-only role, and progress classification machine-checkable JSON fields.

old_accident_surfaces.current_markdown_surface: Markdown goal and plan fixtures separate the single done condition from not-done terminal reports and action-that-can-make-it-done evidence.
old_accident_surfaces.json_port_groundwork: Encode one done condition, allowed achieved claim, non-achieved report protocol, action attempted/proof-of-impossibility, and final report fields in JSON.

old_accident_surfaces.current_markdown_surface: Markdown requirements/goal/plan fixtures encode work covered in this run, action limits, prepare-only handling, forbidden-not-executed handling, and review checks.
old_accident_surfaces.json_port_groundwork: Represent approved horizon, action authority, runtime handling, counts-as-achieved classification, and horizon review status as strict JSON fields.

old_accident_surfaces.current_markdown_surface: Markdown artifact fixtures and templates are linted for placeholder pollution, duplicate content, response-only prompts, jargon leakage, and size-budget issues.
old_accident_surfaces.json_port_groundwork: Keep JSON artifacts concise, schema-owned, free of response-only prompt text, and tied to retained reviewable evidence rather than raw output dumps.

failure_modes.failure_class: Markdown accepted as official guard/compiler/runtime control input.
control_sources: retired design control note for S8
retired goal control note for what counts as done
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future JSON guard/compiler/runtime tests must prove that official requirements.md/design.md/goal.md/plan.md/review.md inputs are refused as control inputs while historical Markdown remains non-authoritative documentation.
future_assertions: Passing path: complete run-directory JSON control chain is accepted.
Failing path: Markdown control artifact passed as official input is rejected with a hard-cut diagnostic.

failure_modes.failure_class: A JSON sidecar is accepted while Markdown remains the authoritative control source.
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future tests must reject a partial sidecar migration where machine keys exist in JSON but semantic control facts are still read from Markdown.
future_assertions: Failing path: sidecar-only requirements.control.json plus Markdown semantics is not a valid official JSON chain.
Passing path: semantic fields and registry bindings are both present in the JSON control files.

failure_modes.failure_class: Runtime mutates approved requirements/design/goal/plan/review/runtime JSON instead of writing progress/status/report files only.
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future runtime/verifier tests must detect any write attempt or content drift in approved control JSON and allow writes only to progress.jsonl, runtime-status.json, and final-report.json.
future_assertions: Failing path: approved control JSON changes after runtime start.
Passing path: progress/status/final-report files change while approved JSON hashes remain stable.

failure_modes.failure_class: goal_achieved true is accepted without verifier permission.
control_sources: retired design control note for S7
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future verifier tests must reject final-report.json with goal_achieved true unless verifier output explicitly permits the completion claim for the approved JSON chain.
future_assertions: Failing path: final report sets goal_achieved true with missing, stale, or negative verifier result.
Passing path: final report can claim achieved only when verifier result permits it.

failure_modes.failure_class: A weaker answer method or forbidden substitute replaces the approved answer method.
control_sources: retired design control note for S9
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future JSON regressions must validate answer_method_key, forbidden_substitute_key, mandatory answer-path nodes, and design-answer-method review status from JSON.
future_assertions: Failing path: JSON chain chooses a forbidden weaker method or omits mandatory answer-path nodes.
Passing path: approved method key, mandatory nodes, and review check are present and coherent.

failure_modes.failure_class: Supporting-only work is counted as goal progress or required-step completion.
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future JSON progress tests must require explicit mainline/supporting-only roles and reject progress records that satisfy required steps using supporting-only work alone.
future_assertions: Failing path: supporting-only task is marked as counts_as_goal_progress true.
Passing path: required-step status changes only through mainline transition evidence.

failure_modes.failure_class: A partial, blocked, diagnostic, unavailable, invalid, fallback, or not-done report is treated as success.
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future final-report schema and verifier tests must separate goal_achieved false reporting from the single achieved condition and reject alternate success statuses.
future_assertions: Failing path: not-done report status is accepted as achieved.
Passing path: not-done report remains goal_achieved false with reason and smallest next action.

failure_modes.failure_class: Approved work coverage is silently shrunk into a future roadmap, handoff, or out-of-scope item because direct execution authority is limited.
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future JSON plan/final-report tests must account for each approved horizon item as executed, prepared-only, forbidden-not-executed, unavailable, or explicitly out-of-scope by what the user approved.
future_assertions: Failing path: a covered item is moved to future roadmap or handoff without explicit approved exclusion.
Passing path: every covered item has a runtime handling classification and final report accounting.

failure_modes.failure_class: Required independent review checks are missing, failed, stale, or not rerun after substantive artifact changes.
failure_modes.json_regression_requirement: Future JSON review tests must validate required review check results, independence flags, post-review mutation status, and final observer approval before runtime compilation or achieved reporting.
future_assertions: Failing path: review status says approved while a required check is absent, failed, stale, or not rerun after mutation.
Passing path: review JSON records all required checks and final observer state as approved.

s9_s10_support.supports_s9_by: Listing the exact old accident classes and future JSON assertions that must be ported once schema/protocol/verifier implementation exists.
s9_s10_support.supports_s10_by: Providing a stable manifest that final integration can inspect as regression-inventory evidence.
